name: Greek-Style Stuffed Acorn Squash
method: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
Position the squash pieces, split side up, on a wide baking tray; roast
for around thirty minutes until they can only be penetrated with a
To chill, transfer to a cutting board.
In the meantime, in a large saucepan, put the lentils; add water to fill.
Just get it to a boil.
Reduce heat; simmer until tender, sealed, for 25 minutes. Withdraw
and put aside.
Simmer the chicken stock in the same frying pan. Insert orzo; cook al
dente according to the box instructions.
Cook meat, crumbling beef, once no longer yellow, eight minutes in a
wide skillet; rinse.
To the skillet, insert lentils and orzo; extract from the heat. Add feta
cheese and Greek spices to two tablespoons; blend well.
Return the stored chicken broth to the saucepan.
Stir in the starch over medium-high heat until it thickens, then
mix into the meat combination.
Quarter squash when cold enough to treat and return to the
baking sheet.
Cover the mixture of meat. Bake for 30 minutes, until the
squash is soft.
Stir with french-fried vegetables and, if necessary, extra feta
before eating.
